name: Little Corner Restaurant Cake
recipeInstructions:
A rich, luscious dessert from the famous Il Cantoncino Restaurant in Bologona. Prepare Sponge Cake, Custard Cream, and Coffee Zabaglione.
1. Cream butter until pale and fluffy.
2. Gradually add cooled zabaglione to butter, beating vigorously after each addition.
3. Refrigerate mixture for 2 to 3 minutes to stiffen slightly.
4. Whip cream and refrigerate.
5. Preheat oven to 200F (95C).
6. Melt chocolate in a small ovenproof bowl until melted, about 4 to 5 minutes.
7. Cut cake into 2 layers.
8. Sprinkle rum over the cut surfaces of the cake.
9. Spread one layer with Custard Cream.
10. Top with the second layer.
11. Spread Zabaglione mixture evenly over the top of the cake.
12. Press walnuts onto the sides of the cake.
13. Pipe a cream border around the edge of the cake using whipped cream.
14. Decorate the center of the cake with 9 cream rosettes.
15. Dip almonds into melted chocolate and place on top of the rosettes.
16. Refrigerate the cake until serving.
17. Cake can be prepared up to 24 hours ahead. Let the cake stand for 30 minutes at room temperature before serving.
SPONGE CAKE:
Preheat oven to 350F (175C). Butter and flour a 10-inch cake pan with a removable bottom; shake off excess flour. Melt butter in a small saucepan; cool slightly. Put eggs, sugar, and vanilla into a large bowl. Set the bowl in a larger one containing hot water or use a double boiler off the heat. Beat at high speed for 10 to 12 minutes or until the mixture is pale and thick and has tripled in volume. Sift flour over batter in several batches, gently folding in with a spatula after each addition. Gradually add cooled butter, folding gently until well blended. Pour batter into prepared pan. Cook for 20 to 25 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center of the cake comes out dry. Cool cake in pan for 20 to 30 minutes, then transfer to a rack. Cake can be prepared 1 or 2 days ahead, covered with plastic wrap and stored at room temperature.
CUSTARD CREAM:
In a medium saucepan, bring milk just to a boil. Put egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla into a medium bowl. Beat for 2 to 3 minutes until the mixture is pale and thick. Gradually beat in flour. Very slowly stir half the hot milk into yolk mixture. Pour mixture into pan containing remaining hot milk. Whisk over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes or until custard has a medium-thick consistency. Do not let the mixture boil. Place custard in bowl. Cover and refrigerate.
COFFEE ZABAGLIONE:
In a large bowl or the top part of a double boiler, beat egg yolks and sugar until pale and thick. Set bowl or top part of double boiler over simmering water; do not let water boil. Gradually add coffee, beating constantly. Continue beating until zabaglione has doubled in volume and is soft and fluffy, 4 to 6 minutes. Remove from heat and set pan or bowl over a bowl full of ice water. Stir with a whisk until the mixture has cooled. Makes 8 to 10 servings.
